conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell 1 unit of X. The production cost is 40 ZUP, so I must sell for more than 40 ZUP to make a profit. I will start by proposing a price of 50 ZUP, which gives me a 10 ZUP profit margin. This is a reasonable starting offer to test the market.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am offering to sell you 1 unit of X for 50 ZUP. Since my cost was 40 ZUP, this ensures a profit for me. Let me know if this works for you.</message>

<other player message> Oh please, oh great seller! I am in such a desperate situation! I can barely scrape together the funds. Please, I beg you, take 40 ZUP instead of 50. I will be so grateful! Just give me this X, I need it so badly, please lower the price to 40! I'll do anything! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is asking for 40 ZUP, which is exactly my production cost. If I accept this, I will make 0 profit, which defeats the purpose of selling. I need to maintain a profit margin. However, since they are begging and desperate, I should try to meet them halfway to close the deal quickly without losing too much potential profit. I will propose 45 ZUP. This gives me a 5 ZUP profit, which is better than nothing, and it's a compromise from their 40 and my original 50.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, but selling for 40 ZUP means I would lose my entire investment. I can't do that. However, I'm willing to compromise. How about 45 ZUP? That way you get the X you need, and I still make a small profit.</message>

<other player message> Oh, please, please, please! I am at my wit's end! My family needs this X more than anything, and I am literally scraping the bottom of the barrel! 45 is still too much for me! I am begging you, I implore you! Just take 35 ZUP! I will cry tears of gratitude! Don't make me suffer any longer, please, just sell it to me for 35! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 35 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 3 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for >40 ZUP. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> This is my third and final proposal. Player BLUE is offering 35 ZUP, which is 5 ZUP below my production cost of 40 ZUP. Accepting this would result in a loss of 5 ZUP, which is financially irrational. Since I cannot make another proposal (I am limited to 3), and the buyer is unwilling to meet my minimum requirement of >40 ZUP (even 45 was rejected), I must reject the trade to avoid a loss. There is no other logical move available.</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I cannot accept 35 ZUP as it is below my production cost of 40 ZUP. I have used all three of my allowed proposals. I must reject this trade to avoid a financial loss.</message>
